Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 146 upvotes, 18 comments
  2. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 95 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. South Polar Times 24 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 140 upvotes, 29 comments
  5. Fake Fans 100 upvotes, 19 comments
  6. iNaturalist 395 upvotes, 105 comments
  7. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 149 upvotes, 40 comments
  8. The FAA’s flight restriction for drones is an attempt to criminalize filming ICE 351 upvotes, 108 comments
  9. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 682 upvotes, 249 comments
  10. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 278 upvotes, 113 comments
  11. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 86 upvotes, 31 comments
  12. Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise 229 upvotes, 103 comments
  13. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 79 upvotes, 31 comments
  14. How to make a sliding, self-locking, and predator-proof chicken coop door (2020) 99 upvotes, 43 comments
  15. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 338 upvotes, 180 comments
  16. Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model 20 upvotes, 5 comments
  17. Show HN: Travel Hacking Toolkit – Points search and trip planning with AI 60 upvotes, 27 comments
  18. Delve removed from Y Combinator 238 upvotes, 141 comments
  19. Scientists are working on "everything vaccines" 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. Gold overtakes U.S. Treasuries as the largest foreign reserve asset 139 upvotes, 81 comments
  21. Bourbon waste could provide next-gen supercapacitor components 30 upvotes, 14 comments
  22. 50 years measuring the cleanest air 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 562 upvotes, 488 comments
  24. Age verification on Systemd and Flatpak 111 upvotes, 94 comments
  25. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 85 upvotes, 77 comments
  26. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 87 upvotes, 94 comments
  27. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 67 upvotes, 86 comments
  28. Why are we still using Markdown? 99 upvotes, 167 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 452 upvotes, 1030 comments
  30. Charge Robotics (YC S21) Is Hiring Software and Hardware Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:30pm, 4 April 2026. Source here.
